{{Quote|Ffib priests! Those fanatical bureaucrats give me chills!|[[Myk'chur Finux Zug]]|<ref name="Star Wars (1998) 14">[[Star Wars (1998) 14|''Star Wars'' (1998) 14]]</ref>}}
The '''Order of the Ffib''', also known as the '''Ffib''', was a theocratic sect based on [[Lorahns/Legends|Lorahns]].

==Beliefs==
The Ffib saw themselves as the only true faith in the galaxy and regarded all others as false. The sect also claimed to be reaching for enlightenment and promised redemption from sins. They were governed by a council of priests based at a [[Great Temple (Lorahns)|Great Temple]] on Lorahns.

The Ffib also sent missionaries off-world to spread their beliefs, resulting in communities on a variety of planets that pledged their loyalty only to the Ffib authority on Lorahns. In some cases, these branches were considered a threat to order on particular planets and have been expelled—sometimes violently. There was a long list of martyrs who had died serving the Ffib order and were honored as holy beings. These missionaries also served as diplomatic representatives and agents of the Ffib, and often participated in espionage and shady dealings, contradicting their beliefs. One such branch were the [[Daughters of the Ffib]].

This theocratic sect was also extremely [[Anti-Jedi sentiment/Legends|anti-Jedi]] due to their refusal to convert to the Ffib sect and the fact that the Order was aware that the Ffib priests and missionaries were charlatans and agents. To support their hatred for the Jedi, the Ffib claimed that the Jedi had been responsible for numerous atrocities which were mainly false. Only one such claim was partially true. The infamous pirate [[Reess Kairn]] - a former Jedi - ransacked the Ffib's Grand Temple, destroying sacred texts, stealing priceless artifacts, and slaying four Ffib priests during his getaway. However, they ignored the fact that Kairn had long ceased to be a Jedi and violated their [[Jedi Code/Legends|principles]] when he perpetrated this act.

==History==
[[File:Reess_Kairn_attacks_Ffib.jpg|thumb|left|300px|[[Reess Kairn]] attacking Ffib's Grand Temple]]

The Ffib were first established during the last centuries of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] on Lorahns. At first, the Ffib were just one of the many cults on the planet, though they eventually converted most of Lorahns's population through the skilled promotion of their beliefs. Thus, Lorahns became one of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] theocracies, with another being the planet [[Monastery/Legends|Monastery]] which was controlled by the [[Order of the Sacred Circle]] and the [[Pius Dea]] sect which ruled the Republic from [[12,000 BBY/Legends|12,000 BBY]] to [[11,000 BBY]] through [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Contispex I/Legends|Contispex I]] and his descendants.

During their reign over the centuries, they suppressed all other forms of religion and ideology on Lorahns, with violations being regarded as [[Treason/Legends|treason]] and punished severely. The Ffib also had their own security force, which they used to keep the planetary population under control. The governments of other planets and systems tolerated the Ffib's rule over Lorahns since they kept the planet stable and non-aggressive.

Desiring galactic domination, the Ffib sent missionaries offworld. These missionaries became a common sight on many worlds and managed to set up communities that were loyal to the Ffib authority on Lorahns. In some cases, these branches were considered a threat to order on particular planets and have been expelled—sometimes violently. There was a long list of martyrs who had died serving the Ffib order. The Ffib honored them as holy beings.

These missionaries also served as diplomatic representatives and agents of the Ffib, and often participated in espionage and shady dealings, contradicting what they preached to their believers. In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], the Ffib order collaborated with the [[Gran Protectorate/Legends|Gran Protectorate]] of [[Malastare/Legends|Malastare]] in a plot to help the [[Lannik (species)/Legends|Lannik]] terrorist group [[Red Iaro/Legends|Red Iaro]] make an [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] attempt on the lawful Lannik ruler and a group of Jedi.

The Ffib survived the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] and were not persecuted by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], possibly due to their hatred of the Jedi. On one occasion, the infamous [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] once earned a 500,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credit]] reward for capturing [[Nivek'Yppiks]], a Ffib heretic wanted by the sect's authorities.
